free_text: Constraining the nuclear spin distribution using
improved 197Au neutron resonance parameters
Experiment performed at the Los
Alamos Neutron Science Center using an early version
of the Device for Indirect Capture Experiments on
Radionuclides (DICER)
Natural Au sample with thickness of 0.00169 at/b
Transmission was measured at newly developed
DICER facility. Neutrons were detected with a 6Li-glass
scintillator 64.4 m from the neutron-production target
Data were taken at the standard- (dt PSR = 125
ns) and reduced-width (dt PSR = 30 ns) proton  pulses
from the proton storage ring (PSR) at LANSCE.
Sample was placed 30 m from the neutron-production
target. Neutrons were detected with a 6Li-glass
scintillator 64.4 m from the neutron- production target
Neutrons were produced by spallation reaction
with 800 MeV protons impinging on tungsten target.
Compiled by S.H.
Transmission data and their uncertainties
were calculated using code SAMMY.
Data shown partly on figs. 1, 2 and 3 of the
reference, sent by author P.E.K.
